title: Tocneppil

{{Quote|Tocneppil doesn't count; he wasn't a Corellian. Besides, I was drunk.|Han Solo|<ref name="ANH Novel" />}}
'''Tocneppil''' was a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] at the height of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|251|Tocneppil}}</ref> a [[Politics/Legends|political]] ideology [[Declaration of a New Order|declared]] with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] formation in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]<ref name="ANH Novel" /> worked with Tocneppil, with the pair once getting lost and not being able to find their [[Starship/Legends|ship]].<ref name="CSWE" /> Solo's partner [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]<ref name="ANH Novel" /> constantly reminded the former about that incident, as Solo claimed that Corellians never got lost.<ref name="CSWE" />

In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Solo and Chewbacca infiltrated the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] to [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|rescue]] one of its [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. When the pair were attempting to find their [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|ship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], Solo justified his claim that Corellians never got lost to Chewbacca by mentioning he was [[Alcohol/Legends|drunk]] during the incident with Tocneppil, and that Tocneppil was not a Corellian.<ref name="ANH Novel" /> Tocneppil was also known to [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], who became the [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on<ref name="ia">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080522/index.html|text="Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array"|int=Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]<ref name="NEC" /> in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].<ref name="date note">{{Tales|3|Lady Luck (comic story)}} establishes that [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] became [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which corresponds to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> More than a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after becoming Baron Administrator, Calrissian sometimes missed traveling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] with Tocneppil.<ref name="ia" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tocneppil first appeared in the [[1976]] [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|novelization]] of the [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']],<ref name="ANH Novel" /> ghostwritten by [[Alan Dean Foster]].<ref name="ANH novel SE">[[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' novelization]], [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] foreword by [[George Lucas]]</ref> According to Foster, Tocneppil's name is a [[tuckerization]] of [[Charles Lippincott|Charlie Lippincott]], who was at the time the publicity supervisor for [[Lucasfilm]].<ref name="Bookshelf">{{InsiderCite|50|Bookshelf}}</ref>
